methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Humanoid, a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Artem Sokolov, has raised $50M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector comprises 11 private peers and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ); notable private companies in the space include 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method here. Using total capital raised ($50M) as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a standard 2.5x multiplier for progression to the subsequent funding stage yields an implied valuation of $125M. This method reflects typical step-up dynamics in robotics venture financing. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible; while 11 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrower filters—same-stage, adjacent-stage, or comparable-tier—produced the minimum three datapoints required for statistical reliability.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $106M to $144M, with a median point of $125M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of a sufficiently dense peer cohort at identical stage and valuation tier, which limits cross-validation; the reliance on a single method also constrains confidence. Investors should treat this estimate as preliminary pending additional comparable transaction data or a later-stage funding event that would enable multi-method triangulation.
